rulings.Case Title: JOSE CERON ET AL VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Seeking Leave To File 4th Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 22, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT SALLY LIU, JOHN LIU Motion Seeking Leave To File 4th Amended Cross-Complaint continued to August 22, 2019. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of opposition to department 501 no later than July 23, 2019 by 4:00 p.m. with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: REAL MANAGEMENT COMPANY VS. CHUI FUNG YU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 22, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT CHUI YU DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint is not uncertain as to Plaintiff's position regarding defendant's evidence as to the occupancy of the unit. Plaintiff's view that evidence provided at most shows a licensee status, not a tenancy, is clear and capable of being responded to. The Court will not be determining the validity of Plaintiff's position regarding the described evidence on a demurrer. Further, while Plaintiff's position that no notice under SFRO 37.9(c) is required to pursue SFRO 37.(a)(7) eviction is not supported by any authority, there can be no demurrer to only a part of a cause of action. A sole cause of action for unlawful detainer is sufficiently asserted in the operative complaint based on a licensee-licensor relationship. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: VOZIKES 2017 LLC VS. JAZDALE INVESTMENTS LLC RELEAF HERBAL COOPERATIVE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 22, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT JAZDALE INVESTMENTS LLC, RELEAF HERBAL COOPERATIVE, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend in part, hearing required in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff: (1) to allege facts showing how a 30 day notice of termination served on April 26, 2019 expired on May 31, 2019. The Court takes judicial notice that there are 35 calendar days between April 26 and May 31; (2) to allege facts (not conclusions) not contradicted by exhibits attached to the complaint (see paragraph 6 vs. Ex.1), establishing the requisite relationship with Releaf; (3) to allege facts showing that service on Releaf via agent for process of service as opposed to the provisions of the Lease was proper; and (4) to check box 2(b) or explain why the box is not checked in light of the "dba" assertions in the lease. Hearing Required to address how the tenant is to determine the date of the termination of tenancy given that the Notice states that termination is "thirty das after service of this notice," i.e. what information is available to Defendant to determine the date of service when service is accomplished by sub-service and mailing? = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: VOZIKES 2017 LLC VS. JAZDALE INVESTMENTS LLC RELEAF HERBAL COOPERATIVE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 22, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT JAZDALE INVESTMENTS LLC, RELEAF HERBAL COOPERATIVE, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that complaint is subject to being stricken under CCP 436(b) for failure to comply with Gov't Code and CRC designed to facilitate the clerk's duties (which in this case have been fulfilled). = (501/CFH)
